title: Perfectly Roasted Irish Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Irish stew featuring roasted green and red lentils in a rich lacquer glaze, perfect for a sick day or a chilly evening. This dish is easy to make and packed with nutrients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the green and red lentils with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the lentils on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are lightly browned and fragrant.
In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onions and minced garlic until they are softened and translucent.
Add the chopped carrots, lacquer glaze, tomato paste, thyme, and roasted lentils to the pot, stirring to combine.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the stew has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Garnish with fresh thyme or parsley, if desired.
